verdict_reasoning: IGF1 is a secreted growth factor (insulin-family neuropeptide) processed from a precursor and released into the extracellular space, where it circulates and binds to cell-surface receptors (IGF1R, others) in transient non-covalent equilibrium. No membrane-anchored isoform, GPI anchor, covalent surface tether, or documented MHC-presentation as a clinically engaged antigen. The protein does not remain stably on the outer leaflet via its own mechanism.
